Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to analyze a provided event log of a Procure to Pay P2P process identify anomalies unusual or problematic sequences and explain why they are problematic based on standard business practices

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Standard P2P Process Before looking at the data I need to recall or define the typical flow of a P2P process Standard Flow Create Purchase Requisition Internal request Approve Purchase Requisition Management authorization Issue Purchase Order Formal order sent to vendor Receive Goods Physical or digital receipt of items Vendor Invoice Received Bill from vendor Match Invoice to PO 3 Way Match Verify PO GR and IV align Release Payment Pay the vendor Close Case Finalize the transaction

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Key Constraints Logic You cannot order before you request approve You generally should not pay before receiving goods or matching the invoice risk of paying for nothing You generally should not receive goods before issuing a PO unless it is an emergency blanket order but standard P2P implies PO first You should not match an invoice before the invoice arrives Approval usually happens before the PO is issued

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Analyze Each Case Individually Case 1001 PR Approve PR Issue PO Receive Goods Invoice Received Match Pay Close Observation This looks like the textbook definition of a standard P2P process Verdict Normal

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1002 PR Issue PO Invoice Received Release Payment Approve PR Receive Goods Match Close Observations Issue Purchase Order happens before Approve Purchase Requisition Release Payment happens before Receive Goods Release Payment happens before Match Invoice to PO Approve Purchase Requisition happens after Release Payment and Receive Goods Verdict Highly Anomalous Multiple control failures

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1003 PR Approve PR Issue PO Release Payment Invoice Received Receive Goods Match Close Observations Release Payment happens before Receive Goods Release Payment happens before Vendor Invoice Received Release Payment happens before Match Invoice to PO Verdict Anomalous Payment made prematurely

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 PR Approve PR Receive Goods Issue PO Invoice Received Match Pay Close Observations Receive Goods happens before Issue Purchase Order Verdict Anomalous Goods received before the legal order was placed

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Synthesize and Structure the Findings Start with a brief introduction acknowledging the standard P2P flow Go through the anomalous cases one by one 1002 1003 1004 For each case list the specific sequence violations Explain the business risk associated with each violation eg fraud compliance financial loss Conclude with a summary of the overall health of the process based on this log

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Refining Case 1002 This is the worst one Payment before approval That is a major internal control violation Payment before goods received Risk of non delivery Refining Case 1003 Payment before invoice and goods This is essentially paying a vendor blindly High fraud risk Refining Case 1004 Goods before PO This is often called a blind receipt It bypasses the negotiation and pricing control of the PO Case 1001 Mention it as the baseline for comparison

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Review against the Prompt Did I identify anomalies Yes Did I explain why they are problematic Yes Did I rely on general P2P knowledge Yes Is the format clear Yes

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Final Polish Ensure the tone is professional and analytical Self Correction Make sure to explicitly mention the Three Way Match concept as it is central to P2P controls
